concerns.text: Task T027 implements 'Mean Signed Correlation (MSC)' as the primary metric. This contradicts FR-005 and SC-002 which explicitly mandate 'Mean Absolute Correlation (MAC)'. The plan.md notes a 'override' but no such amendment exists in spec.md or plan.md. This task covers a non-existent requirement (MSC) and fails to cover the actual requirement (MAC).

Task T036 generates a sensitivity curve of 'p-values vs motion thresholds'. SC-005 explicitly requires the sensitivity curve to measure 'the proportion of subjects retained across motion thresholds'. The task implements the wrong metric, leaving SC-005 uncovered.

Task T034 states 'apply FDR correction to the global mean metric if tested'. FR-008 and FR-011 mandate FDR correction specifically for 'edge-wise statistical testing' (multiple edges). The global mean is a single value. This task misinterprets the requirement, leaving the specific edge-wise FDR requirement (FR-011) inadequately covered and potentially implementing an unrequired correction on the global metric.

Task T035 only stores the 'standard deviation of the permutation null distribution'. SC-002 requires measuring the 'stability of the connectivity strength metric' *against* this value. The task lacks the logic to perform the comparison/verification, leaving the success criterion partially uncovered.

Task T037 specifies 'enforce associational framing'. FR-007 and SC-003 explicitly require the output report to contain the *literal string* 'associational' when randomization is unverified. The task description is vague and does not guarantee the specific wording required by the spec.
